critic_raw:
The preprocessing description states: 'Preprocessing (tokenization, image resizing, normalization) ensures consistency by fitting on the entire dataset before splitting.' This is a canonical data leakage error. For a customer satisfaction prediction model, image normalization statistics and text tokenization vocabulary fitted on the full dataset incorporate test-set distribution information into training preprocessing. While the performance impact may be modest for e-commerce reviews compared to clinical data, the methodological error remains — and the design's own stated validation section references 'months 17-20' as the validation set, implying temporal awareness that is inconsistent with the random split described in Section 2.

The split strategy has an internal contradiction: Section 2 describes a purely random split ('randomly dividing the whole dataset into training 60%, validation 20%, test 20%, without constraints on temporal ordering'), but Section 6 references 'validation set (months 17-20)' for early stopping. These are inconsistent — random splits do not produce temporally ordered validation periods.

For an e-commerce satisfaction prediction task where products, customer demographics, and purchasing behaviors shift over time, temporal ordering of train-test splits is important for deployment validity. A model trained on months 13-24 reviews and tested on months 1-12 reviews cannot be validated against future deployment conditions.

The baseline (text-only model) receives text embeddings from the same BERT architecture as the multimodal model, but the comparison conflates multimodal learning with the additional information from image features. No ablation tests whether image features add value beyond metadata alone, or whether the performance gain from the full multimodal model is primarily driven by metadata (category, price tier, promotional flag) rather than visual content.
all_issues_raised: Data leakage: preprocessing transformers including image normalization and tokenization fitted on entire dataset before splitting
Internal contradiction: Section 2 specifies temporally unordered random split but Section 6 references validation set as 'months 17-20', implying temporal structure
Temporal ordering violated for e-commerce review prediction — random split prevents detecting model performance on future purchasing cohorts
Confounded ablation: baseline is text-only but no metadata-only or image-only ablation planned, so multimodal benefit source cannot be identified
Image features from frozen ResNet-50 (ImageNet-pretrained) may not meaningfully capture product image quality — no validation that image embeddings encode satisfaction-relevant information

Preprocessing leakage: image normalization and other preprocessing statistics are fitted on the full dataset before splitting, allowing test-set distributional information to influence training preprocessing

Temporal structure ignored in split: stratified random sampling across 24 months ignores temporal drift in customer satisfaction patterns due to platform changes, seasonal effects, and review platform maturation

Unfair multimodal vs. text-only comparison: the multimodal model receives product images while the text-only baseline does not, making it impossible to attribute performance differences to architecture rather than additional image modality access

Regression target scale and distribution unspecified: without knowing the distribution of customer satisfaction scores (bounded/unbounded, skewed, presence of ceiling/floor effects), metric choice and loss function cannot be validated

Missing temporal drift analysis: with 24 months of e-commerce data, concept drift in customer satisfaction baselines (seasonal peaks, platform changes, category expansion) is a significant confound that is neither modeled nor analyzed
Preprocessing transformers (image normalization statistics, text tokenization) fit on the entire dataset before splitting, introducing preprocessing leakage from validation and test records
Stratified random split on 24 months of timestamped review data ignores temporal ordering, allowing the model to train on future reviews and exploit temporal trends not available prospectively
Internal contradiction: Section 2 describes a random split across all months, but Section 6 references 'validation set (months 17-20)' — suggesting a temporal split rather than random

Baseline model receives only text features while the multimodal model also receives image and metadata; metadata features are non-imaging and should be included in the baseline to isolate the imaging contribution specifically
No patient or user-level deduplication: the same customer may submit multiple reviews across 24 months, potentially appearing in both train and test sets
